title: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock tumbled 9.0% due to broad-based semiconductor sector collapse triggered by hot CPI data and South Korea's proposed AI dividend threatening memory chip gains.
Dropped 8.6% as memory chip stocks were hit hard by concerns over South Korea's 'AI Citizen Dividend' and broader semiconductor weakness.
Slid 8.9% as part of the semiconductor complex collapse driven by inflation concerns and rate-hold expectations.
Down 5.5% as chip equipment names followed semiconductor stocks lower amid market selloff.
Declined 5.3% as chip equipment sector weakened alongside semiconductor stocks.
Lower by 3.6% as semiconductor-related stocks declined broadly.
Fell 7.6% as memory and storage chip stocks were pressured by sector-wide weakness.
Sank 13% on continuation of post-earnings slide amid semiconductor sector collapse.
Jumped 18% after Q1 adjusted EPS of $4.75 crushed consensus of $4.33 and management raised full-year guidance.
Ripped 15% after beating Q1 EPS and revenue expectations, with catalyst being a new franchise agreement to develop 1,000 restaurants in China.
Climbed 10% after Q2 adjusted EPS beat consensus and management raised fiscal 2026 organic revenue growth guidance.
Surged 14% after reporting Q1 results and raising full-year guidance in its first earnings report as a public company.
Collapsed 33% after slashing full-year 2026 revenue guidance significantly below consensus expectations.
Tumbled 17% after Q4 EPS missed expectations and fiscal 2027 guidance pointed to continued revenue declines and weak profit.
Tumbled 17% after Q4 EPS missed expectations and fiscal 2027 guidance pointed to continued revenue declines and weak profit.
Fell 13% after Q1 revenue missed consensus by wide margin and management flagged a $155-160 million asset write-off due to satellite loss.
Dropped 11% as fiscal Q2 revenue missed Street estimates and was flat year-over-year, despite EPS beat and raised outlook.

title: Ralliant Corporation (NYSE: RAL) Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses
author: Johnson Fistel, Pllp
description: Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating whether Ralliant Corporation complied with federal securities laws following the company's disclosure of a $1.4 billion goodwill impairment in its Test & Measurement segment related to the EA Elektro-Automatik acquisition. The impairment reflected revised expectations for the EA business and reduced EV adoption forecasts. Ralliant's stock price declined over 30% on February 5, 2026, following the earnings announcement. The law firm is seeking investors who suffered losses to join the investigation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company disclosed a $1.4 billion non-cash goodwill impairment, faced a 30% stock price decline, and is now subject to a federal securities investigation regarding potential non-compliance with securities laws and investor losses.

title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Ralliant Corporation - RAL
author: Pomerantz Llp
description: Pomerantz LLP is investigating Ralliant Corporation for potential securities fraud following the company's announcement of a $1.4 billion non-cash goodwill impairment in its Test & Measurement segment related to the EA Elektro-Automatik acquisition. The impairment was attributed to revised long-term expectations and reduced industry forecasts for EV adoption. Ralliant's stock price fell 31.79% on February 5, 2026, dropping $17.89 per share to close at $38.39.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company faces securities fraud investigation, recorded a massive $1.4 billion goodwill impairment, and experienced a significant 31.79% stock price decline due to revised downward expectations for EV adoption and the acquired EA business performance.

title: NYSE: RAL Investigation Alert: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Ralliant Corporation (NYSE: RAL) Investors to Contact the Firm
author: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, Llp
description: Law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check is investigating potential securities law violations by Ralliant Corporation following the company's announcement of a $1.4 billion goodwill impairment charge in its Test & Measurement segment. The disclosure triggered a sharp 31.8% stock price decline on February 5, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company disclosed a substantial $1.4 billion non-cash goodwill impairment charge and is now subject to a securities investigation by a major plaintiff law firm, indicating potential accounting irregularities or misleading prior disclosures. The 31.8% stock price decline reflects severe investor loss of confidence.

title: RAL INVESTOR ALERT: Kirby McInerney LLP Investigates Potential Claims Involving Ralliant Corporation
author: Kirby Mcinerney Llp
description: Law firm Kirby McInerney LLP is investigating Ralliant Corporation for potential federal securities law violations following the company's announcement of a $1.4 billion non-cash goodwill impairment charge in its Test & Measurement segment, primarily related to the EA Elektro-Automatik business. The disclosure triggered a sharp 31.8% stock price decline on February 4, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company announced a substantial $1.4 billion goodwill impairment charge indicating significant overvaluation of acquired assets, triggering a 31.8% stock price decline and prompting a securities investigation into potential violations of federal securities laws and undisclosed business problems.
